annotations.label: Covariance and contravariance

anchor.snippet: describe how the quantitative description of certain geometric or physical entities changes with a change of basis

annotations.note: This is an informal umbrella description of transformation behavior, not a single formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contravariant vs covariant vector

anchor.snippet: a contravariant vector is a list of numbers that transforms oppositely to a change of basis

annotations.note: Vectors (elements of M) and covectors (Module.Dual R M) exist, but the 'transforms oppositely' characterization is not a single stated lemma.

annotations.label: Vector as contravariant tensor

anchor.snippet: has components that transform inversely to the transformation of the reference axes

annotations.note: The component change-of-basis law is captured by toMatrix_mulVec_repr, but the 'inverse to the axes' tensor framing is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Covector

anchor.snippet: a covector has components that transform like the reference axes

annotations.note: A covector is exactly an element of Module.Dual R M = M →ₗ[R] R.

annotations.label: Scalar (invariant)

anchor.snippet: is an object that does not vary with the change in basis

annotations.note: Basis-independence of scalars is not formalized as a named object or statement.

annotations.label: Duality pairing

anchor.snippet: It is the duality pairing of vectors and covectors

annotations.note: Module.dualPairing R M is the evaluation pairing Dual R M →ₗ M →ₗ R (now deprecated as it is literally LinearMap.id/application).

annotations.label: Change of basis transformation

anchor.snippet: refers to how the components of a coordinate vector transform under a change of basis

annotations.note: Basis.toMatrix encodes the change-of-basis/transition matrix, with toMatrix_mulVec_repr giving the component transformation.

annotations.label: Components of a vector in a basis

anchor.snippet: known as the components of v in the f basis

annotations.note: Basis.repr gives the coordinate components of a vector in a basis.

annotations.label: Contravariant transformation rule

anchor.snippet: these components are said to transform contravariantly under a change of basis

annotations.note: toMatrix_mulVec_repr states b'.toMatrix b *ᵥ b.repr m = b'.repr m, i.e. the contravariant transformation of components.

annotations.label: Linear functional components

anchor.snippet: A linear functional α on V is expressed uniquely in terms of its components

annotations.note: A functional expands uniquely in the dual basis (Basis.dualBasis), with components given by its values on the basis.

annotations.label: Covariant transformation rule

anchor.snippet: these components are said to transform covariantly under a change of basis

annotations.note: Dual-basis infrastructure exists, but the explicit covariant (non-inverse) transformation law for covector components is not a stated lemma.

annotations.label: Coordinate functions

anchor.snippet: defines uniquely a set of coordinate functions on V

annotations.note: Basis.coord i : M →ₗ[R] R is the i-th coordinate function of a basis.

anchors.snippet: defines uniquely a set of coordinate functions on V

annotations.label: Contravariant vector (transforms like coordinates)

anchor.snippet: defines a contravariant vector (or simply vector)

annotations.note: The vector object and its component transformation are present, but 'contravariant vector' as a transformation-defined notion is not a named definition.

anchors.snippet: defines a contravariant vector (or simply vector)

annotations.label: Covariant vector (covector)

anchor.snippet: is then a covariant vector (or covector)

annotations.note: A covariant vector/covector is an element of Module.Dual R M.

anchors.snippet: is then a covariant vector (or covector)

annotations.label: Frame related by inverse Jacobian

anchor.snippet: is related to the frame f by the inverse of the Jacobian matrix of the coordinate transition

annotations.note: Transition matrices between bases are mutually inverse (toMatrix_mul_toMatrix), but the manifold coordinate-frame/Jacobian statement itself is not formalized this way.

anchors.snippet: is related to the frame f by the inverse of the Jacobian matrix of the coordinate transition

annotations.label: Tangent vector

anchor.snippet: A tangent vector is by definition a vector that is a linear combination of the coordinate partials

annotations.note: Tangent vectors are elements of TangentSpace I x, though Mathlib defines this abstractly rather than via coordinate partials.

anchors.snippet: A tangent vector is by definition a vector that is a linear combination of the coordinate partials

annotations.label: Contravariant vector on a manifold

anchor.snippet: transform in this way on passing from one coordinate system to another is called a contravariant vector

annotations.note: The tangent bundle (whose transition functions are coordinate Jacobians) exists, but 'contravariant vector' defined by its transformation law is not stated.

annotations.label: Metric identifies vectors with covectors

anchor.section: Covariant and contravariant components of a vector with a metric
anchor.snippet: because the bilinear form allows covectors to be identified with vectors

annotations.note: BilinForm.toDual is the linear equivalence V ≃ₗ Module.Dual K V induced by a nondegenerate bilinear form.

anchors.section: Covariant and contravariant components of a vector with a metric
anchors.snippet: because the bilinear form allows covectors to be identified with vectors

annotations.label: Reciprocal basis

anchor.section: Covariant and contravariant components of a vector with a metric
anchor.snippet: there is a unique reciprocal basis

annotations.note: BilinForm.dualBasis is the unique reciprocal basis with B(dualBasis i)(b j) = δ_ij.

anchors.section: Covariant and contravariant components of a vector with a metric
anchors.snippet: there is a unique reciprocal basis

annotations.label: Contravariant and covariant components

anchor.section: Covariant and contravariant components of a vector with a metric
anchor.snippet: are the contravariant components of the vector v in the basis f

annotations.note: Contravariant components are Basis.repr and covariant components are (B.dualBasis).repr x i = B x (b i) by dualBasis_repr_apply.

anchors.section: Covariant and contravariant components of a vector with a metric
anchors.snippet: are the contravariant components of the vector v in the basis f

annotations.label: Dual basis in Euclidean plane

anchor.snippet: the dot product allows for vectors to be identified with covectors

annotations.note: toDualMap is the inner-product isometric embedding x ↦ ⟪x,·⟫ identifying vectors with covectors.

anchors.snippet: the dot product allows for vectors to be identified with covectors

annotations.label: Dual basis perpendicularity

anchor.snippet: are perpendicular to each other

annotations.note: apply_dualBasis_left gives B(dualBasis i)(b j) = δ_ij, encoding the orthogonality of reciprocal basis vectors to off-index basis vectors.

annotations.label: Dual basis of a 45° pair

anchor.snippet: consisting of a pair of vectors making a 45° angle with one another

annotations.note: This specific 2D numerical worked example is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual basis in 3D Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: one can also determine explicitly the dual basis to a given set of basis vectors

annotations.note: The general reciprocal basis exists, but the explicit 3D cross-product/triple-product formula for it is not formalized.

annotations.label: Mutually reciprocal bases

anchor.snippet: they are still mutually reciprocal

annotations.note: dualBasis_dualBasis_flip shows taking the reciprocal basis twice returns the original, i.e. the bases are mutually reciprocal.

annotations.label: Contravariant components via dot product

anchor.snippet: the contravariant components of any vector v can be obtained by the dot product of v with the dual basis vectors

annotations.note: The reciprocal basis exists, but the identity v^i = B(v, dualBasis^i) for contravariant components is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Covariant components via dot product

anchor.snippet: the covariant components of v can be obtained from the dot product of v with basis vectors

annotations.note: dualBasis_repr_apply states the covariant component (B.dualBasis).repr x i = B x (b i), i.e. v dotted with the basis vector.

annotations.label: Orthonormal basis equals dual basis

anchor.snippet: If the basis vectors are orthonormal , then they are the same as the dual basis vectors

annotations.note: OrthonormalBasis exists and satisfies ⟪e_i,e_j⟫=δ_ij, but a lemma stating its reciprocal basis equals itself was not found.

annotations.label: Covariant basis (D.1)

anchor.snippet: Covariant basis of a vector space of dimension n

annotations.note: A covariant basis is just a Module.Basis of the vector space.

annotations.label: Contravariant components (D.2)

anchor.snippet: Contravariant components of a vector

annotations.note: Contravariant components are the coordinates Basis.repr of a vector in the (covariant) basis.

annotations.label: Dual (contravariant) basis (D.3)

anchor.snippet: Dual (contravariant) basis of a vector space of dimension n

annotations.note: The metric/reciprocal dual basis is BilinForm.dualBasis (and the purely algebraic dual is Basis.dualBasis).

annotations.label: Covariant components (D.4)

anchor.snippet: Covariant components of a vector

annotations.note: Covariant components v_i = B x (b i) are given by dualBasis_repr_apply.

annotations.label: Covariant metric tensor (D.5)

anchor.snippet: Components of the covariant metric tensor

annotations.note: g_ij = B(e_i, e_j) is exactly BilinForm.toMatrix (toMatrix_apply) of the metric bilinear form in a basis.

annotations.label: Contravariant metric tensor (D.6)

anchor.snippet: Components of the contravariant metric tensor

annotations.note: The covariant metric matrix is BilinForm.toMatrix, but its inverse g^{ij} (contravariant metric) is not packaged as a named declaration.

annotations.label: Valence of a tensor

anchor.snippet: The valence of a tensor is the number of covariant and contravariant terms

annotations.note: Tensor products exist, but the notion of tensor valence/type (p,q) is not formalized as a named concept.

annotations.label: Raising and lowering indices with the metric

anchor.snippet: Contravariant indices can be turned into covariant indices by contracting with the metric tensor

annotations.note: Lowering an index is the metric isomorphism BilinForm.toDual : V ≃ₗ Dual K V (raising is its inverse), though only the rank-1 case is packaged.

annotations.label: Contravariant vector (tensor analysis)

anchor.snippet: A contravariant vector is one which transforms like

annotations.note: The component transformation law exists, but a 'contravariant vector' defined by transformation behavior is not a named definition.

annotations.label: Covariant vector (tensor analysis)

anchor.snippet: A covariant vector is one which transforms like

annotations.note: A covariant vector is an element of the dual space Module.Dual R M.

annotations.label: Dual space as contravariant functor

anchor.snippet: The assignment of the dual space to a vector space is a standard example of a contravariant functor

annotations.note: The contravariant action on morphisms (Dual.transpose / LinearMap.dualMap) exists, but it is not packaged as a categorical contravariant Functor on ModuleCat.

annotations.label: Covariant components in differential geometry

anchor.snippet: are covariant if they change with the same linear transformation as a change of basis

annotations.note: Dual-basis infrastructure for covector components exists, but the covariant transformation characterization is not stated as a lemma.
